title: Map Command, Page State, Navigation Invariants, and Transactional Updates · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in transactional Pokemon page navigator and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state.
Compute candidate navigation, fetch and validate it, then commit state only after success.
Disable or explain next and back at absent-link boundaries and reject negative, excessive, or mismatched pagination state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Page State Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 4370 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to commit only admitted page transitions while preserving cursor state on failure; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state.

Inspect the Pokedex Go Page State Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Page State Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store current resource, offset, limit, next, previous, result identities, and revision as one explicit page state. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Page State Model, keep Pokedex Go Page State Model connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Write the debug evidence note for Pokedex Go Map Transition Tests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 8322 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to commit only admitted page transitions while preserving cursor state on failure; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
